title: Make `buildah pull` scripting friendly
body:
When `buildah pull` sees images matching options from multiple registries, it stops and prompts the user:
```
# buildah pull goharbor/nginx-photon:v1.17.1

? Please select an image:
  ▸ docker.io/goharbor/nginx-photon:v1.17.1
    quay.io/goharbor/nginx-photon:v1.17.1
```

Something in the style of APT's `-y` flag would be welcomed, so that sequential pulls could be scripted in the style of:
`buildah pull -y goharbor/nginx-photon:v1.17.1`
and the default is accepted automatically.

comments.body:
First, it is not a yes or no question, it is multiple choice so such flag doesn't really make sense.
Second, we only prompt on an interactive tty, so if you run the script in the background we never prompt but in that case an error could be returned depending on the short name options configured see the docs https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-registries.conf.5.md#short-name-aliasing-modes
and especially https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-registries.conf.5.md#note-risk-of-using-unqualified-image-names

If you use this in scripts the simple fix is to always use full names including the registry, that does not require any specific config changes or cli options.
